title: 3/25/19 Google Earth links on Boulder Adventures page

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log for March 25th, Monday, 8.24 a.m. 2019, March 25th.
So, I'm diurnalized. I just woke up not so long ago.
I think I got to sleep at like midnight. I got my eight hours last night, so I'm all charged up and ready to go.
So, today I'm going to work on my Google Earth Boulder Adventures page.
That page right there, what I'm going to do is make it so that these links work.
Oh, whoops. I don't even know why that did that.
So, link them to the different pages and then make sure that, you know, all the links are fixed until I find something that needs to be finished on Google Earth.
Yeah, the project I've been working on for the last week was super time-consuming, but I burned through it pretty quick.
So, I have confidence to continue. I think I'm excited to see how much I can do now that I'm working on the site.
And then, of course, when I hit the next Google Earth project, I'll hit it.
I'll go and dive into it headfirst because I feel like I can take it out because I did these mountains.
I didn't totally finish it, but I did enough.
And, yeah, so, who knows what today's got in store.
I like working on the website because that's where you actually see movement happening.
When you're working on something on Google Earth forever, you don't actually see it moving.
So, you know, it's good to feel like you're making progress.
I'm starting to get a little bit more views on my YouTube, on my vlogs.
I get, like, eight a day.
That goes along with trolls, though.
I've got a top speed 350 troll who's fucking saying annoying shit to me.
They're like, you're not going to get any money when you retire because you're not paying taxes.
I wasn't able to respond to that, though.
I think they deleted it right away, but goddammit, dude.
That bitch needs to shut up.
So, it gets a little bit annoying when you get it, when you have trolls hit you all the time, but it's not.
But if you get to the point where trolls are just coming at you all day, every day, and you're able to comment on them,
At that point, it actually kind of becomes fun because then you're, like, you're interacting with people.
But they never, they don't do two-ways, so I always bounce.
Two-way conversations.
So, I'm going to, oh, I was listening to the Trilateral Commission report on what they think about democracy,
Democracy and crisis or something.
I think I want to put it on my website and just listen to it a bunch of times.
It's, it's the ramblings of crazy billionaires, but they do, they do provide a whole lot of facts and figures.
So, that's interesting to listen to.
And what else is I listening to?
I think I did the, it's bouncing around.
I did, I think I did some more Monson and Plato Republic yesterday, too.
Anyways, I've been talking long enough, don't have that much to say today.
Tomorrow, hopefully by tomorrow, I will, I actually have no idea, but hopefully I'll have a good day.
Maybe I'll be done with this whole Boulder Ventures page, I don't know.
But, all right, let's see, let's see what we can do today.
All right, later.
